reasoning:
The question asks whether at least four of the Magnificent Seven stocks will close 50% below their all-time highs at any point between late 2023/early 2024 and January 1, 2026.

As of October 2025, search results confirm that the "Magnificent Seven" stocks (AAPL, AMZN, GOOG, META, MSFT, NVDA) are trading at or very near all-time highs. For example, on October 9, 2025, AAPL was around $273, AMZN around $226, GOOG near $316, and MSFT near $472. 

Because we only have roughly two months remaining until the resolution date (January 1, 2026), four of these mega-cap stocks would need to suffer a phenomenal and unprecedented 50% collapse from their all-time highs within a span of ~45 trading days. While rapid crashes have occurred (e.g., March 2020, October 1987), a concentrated 50% halving of the 4 largest companies in such a short window is an extreme tail risk that has practically 0% historical base rate, justifying the minimum allowed probability boundary of 0.02. The community forecast accurately reflected this with a prediction of 3% in mid-October.
